items.question: What does the Image to Video AI workflow do?
items.answer: This workflow turns a single image into a short video clip by adding camera motion, ambient movement, and subtle animation. It is designed as an AI image to video generator for social content, ads, and storytelling.

items.question: What kind of input works best for Image to Video?
items.answer: Start with a clear, well-lit image that has a strong subject and composition—such as a product shot, illustration, portrait, or scene. You can optionally add a short text description to guide how the camera should move and what kind of motion you want.

items.question: Can I control how the image moves in the final video?
items.answer: Yes. In your prompt you can describe pans, zooms, parallax, and the overall pacing or mood (for example, slow cinematic zoom, fast social scroll animation, or subtle ambient movement). The workflow uses this description as guidance for the motion it generates.

items.question: What are typical use cases for AI Image to Video?
items.answer: Common use cases include turning static product photos into motion-first creatives for ads, animating key visuals for social feeds, creating motion from illustrations, and adding subtle movement to slides or explainer visuals.

items.question: Can I generate multiple video variations from the same image?
items.answer: You can run the workflow multiple times with different prompts, motion styles, or durations to explore variations. This is useful when testing different cuts for social media or campaigns.

items.question: How does pricing work for Image to Video generations?
items.answer: Each generation consumes credits depending on the model settings you use. A good practice is to start with a few shorter test runs to find a motion style you like, then generate more polished versions once you are happy with the direction.
